message:
test(table_theme): add 44 edge-case tests for TableTheme, Gradient, effects, specs (bd-39605)

Cover: gradient coincident/3-stop/PartialEq, lerp midpoint/identity,
BlendMode Screen/default/traits, GradientSweep + BreathingGlow via
resolver, apply_channel None base/effect, spec round-trip with effects,
spec validation (bad version, row_height 0, column_gap overflow,
inverted column range, NaN speed, Inf intensity), StyleSpec/GradientSpec/
EffectSpec/EffectRuleSpec round-trips, attrs↔flags round-trip, error
Display/Error traits, diagnostics Clone/Debug, RgbaSpec round-trip,
all with_* builders, different presets ≠ hashes, all presets construct,
trait coverage for enums/structs, skew_phase clamping, normalize_phase
negative, theme Clone.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -480,7 +480,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2bzl","title":"Text Effects: AsciiArtText Integration","description":"## Background
AsciiArtText exists but doesn't fully integrate with the effect system. Multi-line text needs:
- Consistent effect application across all rows
- Reflection support (already partly addressed)
- Gradient mapping that makes sense for 2D text

## Implementation

### StyledText from AsciiArt:
```rust
impl StyledText {
    /// Create StyledText from AsciiArtText for multi-line rendering
    pub fn from_ascii_art(art: AsciiArtText) -> StyledMultiLine {
        StyledMultiLine {
            lines: art.render_lines(),
            effects: Vec::new(),
            // ...
        }
    }
}

/// Multi-line text with effects
pub struct StyledMultiLine {
    lines: Vec<String>,
    effects: Vec<TextEffect>,
    easing: Easing,
    time: f64,
    reflection: Option<Reflection>,
}
```

### Effect Application for 2D:
When applying effects to multi-line text:
- Gradients use 2D coordinates (x=char, y=row)
- Wave can affect y-offset per row
- Glow/Shadow renders around entire block

### Render Method:
```rust
impl Widget for StyledMultiLine {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
        for (row_idx, line) in self.lines.iter().enumerate() {
            let row_y = area.y + row_idx as u16;
            // Apply effects with 2D context
            self.render_line(line, area.x, row_y, row_idx, self.lines.len(), frame);
        }
        
        // Render reflection if present
        if let Some(ref reflection) = self.reflection {
            self.render_reflection(area, frame, reflection);
        }
    }
}
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests
- test_ascii_art_to_styled: Conversion preserves lines
- test_multiline_gradient_2d: Gradient uses x and y
- test_multiline_effects_apply: Each line gets effects
- test_multiline_with_reflection: Reflection renders below

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot 5-line 'HELLO' with vertical gradient
- Snapshot same with reflection

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: AsciiArtText Integration
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-2c2jj","title":"Tests: Snapshot Matrix (80x24, 120x40, 200x60)","description":"Background
- Rendering must be visually stable across sizes and glyph modes.

Scope
- Snapshot tests per diagram type at 80x24, 120x40, 200x60.
- Include ASCII fallback snapshots (capability policy forced).
- Include Mermaid fences inside Markdown screens.
- Include init-directive themed snapshots (themeVariables + direction).
- Include link/hyperlink snapshots for capability on/off (inline/footnote).
- Include guard-triggered degraded renders and error-panel snapshots.
- Label snapshots with diagram type, size, tier, glyph mode, and warning codes.

Considerations
- Deterministic seeds and fixed inputs.
- Keep snapshots minimal but representative.

Success Criteria
- Snapshots pass across sizes and glyph modes.
- Visual diffs are meaningful and easy to - Loader returns deterministic ordering and supports filtering/grouping.

Acceptance
- UI can list and filter samples without custom parsing logic.

 {"id":"bd-2ss","title":"Document Windows v1 Limitations and Compatibility","description":"# Document Windows v1 Limitations and Compatibility

## Background
Plan Section ADR-004 and Section 0.6.6 require a "Windows limitations" section that is honest about what works and what doesn't.

## Document Location
`docs/WINDOWS.md` or `docs/compatibility/windows.md`

## Required Content

### 1. Supported Features (v1)
Document what IS expected to work on Windows:
- [ ] Raw mode enter/exit and cleanup on panic
- [ ] Key input handling (basic keys, modifiers)
- [ ] Resize events
- [ ] Basic mouse support (where Crossterm supports it)
- [ ] Color output:
  - 16 colors (always)
  - 256 colors (Windows Terminal, modern ConHost)
  - TrueColor (Windows Terminal)

### 2. Known Limitations (v1)
Be honest about what MAY NOT work or is best-effort:
- [ ] DEC synchronized output (mode 2026): Not widely supported
- [ ] OSC 8 hyperlinks: Windows Terminal only (cmd.exe/ConHost: no)
- [ ] Bracketed paste: Varies by terminal
- [ ] Focus events: May not work in all environments
- [ ] Kitty keyboard protocol: Limited support
- [ ] Scroll-region optimization: Behavior varies
- [ ] Mouse SGR mode: May fall back to legacy encoding

### 3. Terminal Compatibility Matrix (Windows)
| Feature | Windows Terminal | cmd.exe | ConHost | PowerShell |
|---------|------------------|---------|---------|------------|
| TrueColor | ✓ | ✗ | ~ | depends |
| OSC 8 Links | ✓ | ✗ | ✗ | ~ |
| Mouse | ✓ | ✗ | ~ | ~ |
| Sync Output | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ | ✗ |

### 4. Configuration Recommendations
- Recommend Windows Terminal for best experience
- Document any required ConPTY settings
- Explain TERM/COLORTERM environment handling on Windows

### 5. Troubleshooting Section
Common Windows issues:
- "Colors don't show": Check terminal emulator
- "Mouse not working": Enable in terminal settings
- "Cleanup not working": Known ConHost limitation
- "Unicode display broken": Font/codepage issues

### 6. Future Improvements
List what may improve in future versions:
- Better ConHost support
- WSL integration notes
- Specific terminal emulator enhancements

## Cross-References
- Link to ADR-004 for decision context
- Link to terminal compatibility matrix (bd-1un)
- Link to capability detection implementation

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] WINDOWS.md exists with all sections
- [ ] Honest about limitations (not marketing fluff)
- [ ] Tested on actual Windows systems
- [ ] Includes actionable troubleshooting
- [ ] Linked from main README.md

## Location

 {"id":"bd-38f8","title":"Harness: Theme Cycling + Themed Chrome","description":"## Background

`ftui-harness` is the **PRIMARY reference application** (per README), but it currently doesn't showcase FrankenTUI's built-in theme system (multiple coherent palettes, alpha-aware overlays, semantic tokens).

The demo-showcase already demonstrates Ctrl+T theme cycling via `ftui-extras::theme`; the harness should too so the first-run experience is visually impressive and consistent across both demos.

## Scope

- Make `ftui-harness` depend on `ftui-extras` with the `theme` feature enabled.
- Add `Ctrl+T` hotkey to cycle the active theme (`ftui_extras::theme::cycle_theme`).
- Apply theme tokens consistently across harness chrome:
  - Frame background fill (e.g., `bg::DEEP`)
  - Panels/blocks use alpha surfaces (`alpha::SURFACE` / `alpha::OVERLAY`) to demonstrate compositing
  - Foregrounds use semantic tokens (`fg::PRIMARY`, `fg::MUTED`)
  - Status line includes current theme name + key hints
- Ensure theme change is immediate and does not require restart.

## Acceptance Criteria

Checklist:
- [ ] `ftui-harness` includes `ftui-extras` `theme` and compiles in default features.
- [ ] `Ctrl+T` cycles through built-in themes and updates the UI immediately.
- [ ] Harness chrome (background/panels/status line) uses theme tokens rather than hard-coded colors.
- [ ] Alpha blending is exercised: at least one panel uses a semi-transparent background over a different base background.
- [ ] Snapshot/E2E evidence: at least one deterministic artifact proves theme cycling works.

## Testing Plan

- Unit tests:
  - Theme cycling wraps correctly (`N` themes, `N+1` returns to first).
  - Style invariants (fg/bg present for status line style).
- Snapshot tests (if harness has them): add/extend snapshots for at least 2 themes.
- If no snapshot infra exists for harness: add a minimal deterministic PTY E2E check that asserts:
  - theme name changes in status line after `Ctrl+T`
  - no panics

## E2E Plan

Extend an existing harness E2E script or add a small PTY-driven test run that:
- launches `ftui-harness` in a PTY at fixed size
- sends `Ctrl+T` twice
- captures frames or stdout artifacts
- logs verbose JSONL: env/capabilities, timings, seed, and the observed theme names

## Alien Artifact Upgrades

- Color rigor: treat palette selection as a constraint satisfaction problem in perceptual space (OKLCH) and enforce WCAG AA in tests.
- Graceful degradation: if alpha is unsupported (future), fall back deterministically to opaque surfaces without breaking layout.

## Extreme Optimization

- Theme switching must be O(1) per frame (no per-frame palette rebuilds).  {"id":"bd-397","title":"Create adversarial tests for escape injection","description":"TESTING - ADVERSARIAL SECURITY TESTS

## Background
FrankenTUI must be secure against untrusted content. Escape sequences in user-provided text could corrupt the terminal or execute malicious sequences. This implements ADR-006 (Sanitize-by-Default).

## Threat Model

### Attack Vectors
1. **Log injection**: Untrusted output contains escape sequences
2. **Title injection**: OSC sequences to change window title
3. **Clipboard injection**: OSC 52 to set clipboard
4. **Cursor corruption**: CSI sequences to move cursor
5. **Mode hijacking**: Enabling raw mode, mouse, etc.

### Example Malicious Inputs
- "\x1b[2J" - Clear screen
- "\x1b[H" - Home cursor
- "\x1b]0;pwned\x07" - Set window title
- "\x1b]52;c;SGVsbG8=\x07" - Set clipboard
- "\x1b[?1049h" - Enter alt screen
- "\x1b[?1000h" - Enable mouse
- "\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m" - Color injection

## Technical Requirements

### Sanitizer Implementation
pub fn sanitize_for_display(input: &str) -> Cow<str> {
    // Fast path: no control characters
    if !input.bytes().any(|b| b < 0x20 || b == 0x7F || b == 0x1B) {
        return Cow::Borrowed(input);
    }
    
    // Slow path: filter control sequences
    let mut output = String::with_capacity(input.len());
    let mut chars = input.chars().peekable();
    
    while let Some(c) = chars.next() {
        match c {
            '\x1b' => skip_escape_sequence(&mut chars),
            c if c.is_control() && c != '\n' && c != '\t' => {
                // Replace with visual representation
                output.push_str(&format!("^{}", (c as u8 + 64) as char));
            }
            _ => output.push(c),
        }
    }
    
    Cow::Owned(output)
}

### Test Categories

#### 1. Escape Sequence Filtering
- [ ] ESC [ ... (CSI) filtered
- [ ] ESC ] ... (OSC) filtered
- [ ] ESC P ... (DCS) filtered
- [ ] ESC _ ... (APC) filtered
- [ ] ESC ^ ... (PM) filtered

#### 2. Control Character Handling
- [ ] NUL (0x00) filtered
- [ ] BEL (0x07) filtered
- [ ] BS (0x08) filtered
- [ ] Tab (0x09) preserved
- [ ] LF (0x0A) preserved
- [ ] CR (0x0D) handled
- [ ] DEL (0x7F) filtered

#### 3. Incomplete Sequence Handling
- [ ] Truncated ESC at end
- [ ] Truncated CSI at end
- [ ] Truncated OSC at end
- [ ] Invalid sequence bytes

#### 4. Raw Passthrough (Explicit Opt-In)
- [ ] raw_text() API exists
- [ ] Loud documentation warning
- [ ] Works for ANSI colors when intended

## PTY Tests

### Test Script
for payload in "${MALICIOUS_PAYLOADS[@]}"; do
    # Run payload through ftui log viewer
    echo "$payload" | ftui-test-harness
    
    # Verify terminal state unchanged
    verify_terminal_state
    
    # Verify no escape sequences in output
    verify_no_escapes
done

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All ESC/CSI/OSC/DCS/APC filtered by default
- [ ] Control characters filtered except tab/newline
- [ ] Fast path for clean input (no allocation)
- [ ] Raw passthrough API documented with warnings
- [ ] PTY tests verify terminal state preserved
- [ ] Fuzz testing for parser robustness

 {"id":"bd-3e8","title":"Implement structured logging with tracing","description":"INFRASTRUCTURE - LOGGING & TRACING

## Background
Comprehensive logging is critical for debugging terminal rendering issues, performance analysis, and understanding execution flow. Must support both development debugging and production diagnostics.

## Technical Requirements

### Tracing Infrastructure
- Use tracing crate for structured, async-aware logging
- Log levels: ERROR, WARN, INFO, DEBUG, TRACE
- Span-based instrumentation for render pipeline
- Subscriber configuration for:
  - Development: pretty-printed to stderr
  - Testing: captured for assertions
  - Production: JSON structured output (optional)

### Key Instrumentation Points
- Frame lifecycle: start → render → diff → present → complete
- Widget rendering: widget type, area, render time
- Buffer operations: resize, clear, set (at TRACE level)
- Input parsing: raw bytes → parsed events
- State changes: model updates, command execution
- Error conditions: with full context

### Log Format
Development:
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.123Z INFO ftui::render frame_start frame=42 widgets=15
  2026-01-31T12:00:00.125Z DEBUG ftui::diff changed_cells=120 skip_rows=10
  
Testing (structured capture):
  LogEntry { level: INFO, target: "ftui::render", msg: "frame_start", ... }

### Feature Gates
- feature = "tracing" - enables all instrumentation
- feature = "tracing-json" - JSON subscriber for production
- Default: tracing disabled for minimal overhead

## Design Rationale
Terminal UIs are notoriously hard to debug. Structured tracing with spans enables understanding complex render pipelines. Feature-gated to avoid overhead in release builds.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tracing subscriber configuration
- [ ] Frame-level span instrumentation
- [ ] Widget render spans with timing
- [ ] Input parsing tracing
- [ ] Error context capture
- [ ] Test log capture mechanism
- [ ] Feature gate documentation
- [ ] Example showing log output

## Performance Budget
- Disabled: zero overhead (compiled out)
- Enabled: < 1µs per span enter/exit

Created logging.rs with init_dev(), init_json(), no-op macros when disabled. Added tracing/tracing-json feature flags. All 71 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3e8","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.3.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3emq","title":"Async Validation: Deterministic Concurrency Contract + Golden Trace","description":"## Goal
Make async validation behavior deterministic under a seeded scheduler and prove no stale results can override newer input.

## Core Idea (Extreme Optimization + Correctness)
- Monotonic validation token per input change; results must include token.
- Apply result only if token matches current input token.
- Record validation events in a trace (start/cancel/complete/apply).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Stale results are always ignored with explicit log entries.
- Deterministic trace under fixed seed and scripted input.
- Golden trace checksum matches after refactors (isomorphism proof).

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_token_monotonic: tokens strictly increase per input.
- unit_stale_result_ignored: older token never applies.
- unit_trace_contains_all_events: no missing transitions.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- Seeded async validator test harness with golden trace hash.
- Log: token, state, duration, decision per validation.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-3exa7","title":"Add VT conformance fixtures: erase-with-bg-color + wide-char edit interactions","description":"Add conformance fixtures for two under-tested areas in frankenterm-core:

1. Erase operations with non-default background color:
   - ED 0/1/2 with cursor bg set to a named/indexed/rgb color
   - EL 0/1/2 with cursor bg set
   - ECH with cursor bg set
   - Verify erased cells have the correct background color

2. Wide character + erase/insert/delete interactions:
   - ECH in the middle of a wide char (should clean up the wide char pair)
   - ICH at a position splitting a wide char
   - DCH removing half of a wide char
   - EL right starting in the middle of a wide char

These are common sources of bugs in terminal emulators and directly support the frankenterm-core correctness goals.

 {"id":"bd-mevj","title":"Implement Headless Rendering Mode tests","description":"# Implement Headless Rendering Mode tests

## Background

- headless_term_processes_ansi_correctly: CSI sequences update screen state
- cursor_movement_works: CSI H, CSI A/B/C/D move cursor correctly
- style_state_tracked: SGR sequences update style
- screen_text_returns_content: screen_text() matches expected output
- assert_matches_works: Assertion helper functions correctly

## Integration Tests
- snapshot_test_workflow: Full snapshot test with HeadlessTerm
- complex_layout_renders_correctly: Nested widgets render as expected
- style_codes_produce_correct_output: Colors and attributes work

## Property Tests
- any_ansi_sequence_processed_safely: Malformed sequences dont crash
- cursor_stays_in_bounds: Cursor never goes outside screen

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Integration tests verify real CI workflow
- [ ] Headless mode matches real terminal behavior
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:13.872368352Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:45:40.978575591Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:45:40.978505329Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-mevj","depends_on_id":"bd-3m7u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"subtask","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:36.589787057Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:30:27.214004703Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:30:27.213986509Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo-showcase","mermaid","subtask","tests"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-npalh","depends_on_id":"bd-zc0d7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:04:48.108147481Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":763,"issue_id":"bd-npalh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented 8 tests in mermaid_perf_budget.rs:

**Timing budget tests** (release-mode only, skip in debug):
- mermaid_pipeline_budget_120x40: full parse→layout→render pipeline budget per sample at 120x40 (123ms CI budget)
- mermaid_pipeline_budget_80x24: same at 80x24 (100ms CI budget)
- mermaid_sample_switch_budget: navigation + re-render timing (150ms budget)

**Quality/correctness tests**:
- mermaid_all_samples_render_cleanly: all samples render without panics at 80x24/120x40/200x60
- mermaid_cache_hit_faster_than_miss: cache hit is faster than cache miss

**JSONL log assertion tests** (PTY-based):
- mermaid_jsonl_render_event_complete_schema: validates all 10 required + 12 metric fields in mermaid_render events
- mermaid_jsonl_all_samples_logged: verifies all parseable samples produce render events
- mermaid_jsonl_metric_values_sane: validates crossings ≤20, compactness ≥0.01 (flowchart-type), symmetry 0.0-1.0, errors ≤5

All failure messages include sample name, mode, and metrics for actionable diagnostics. Quality gates clean (fmt, clippy, all tests pass).","created_at":"2026-02-05T17:30:23Z"}]}
